primary.justification: The chapter's entire architecture is organised around explaining and analysing the Gupta period as a major historical phase — its political rise, cultural and scientific achievements, and eventual decline — and its profound impact on Indian society. Every named section (Chandragupta I, Samudragupta, Chandragupta II, Faxian's Account, Governance, Thriving Trade, Classical Age, Quest for Beauty, Decline, South and Northeast) addresses either the nature of this historical phase or its societal consequences. Remove this competency and the chapter loses its fundamental organising purpose. History sub-discipline.

The section 'New Ideas and Wonders: The Classical Age' is a dedicated, architecturally distinct section organised entirely around India's contributions to human knowledge — mathematics and astronomy (Āryabhaṭa's axial rotation theory, year-length calculation, eclipse explanation), encyclopedic science (Varāhamihira's Bṛihat Samhitā), Sanskrit literature (Kālidāsa), medicine (Charaka Saṃhitā, Suśhruta Saṃhitā), and metallurgy (the rust-free Iron Pillar). The section directly meets C-10.1's demand and could stand alone as a learning unit on India's contributions to humanity. Rule 5(a) satisfied.
